data.title: NIST Allocates Over $3 Million to Small Businesses Advancing AI, Biotechnology, Semiconductors, Quantum and More
data.summary: NIST is allocating funding to eight small businesses in seven states under the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) program.

data.title: NIST Launches Centers for AI in Manufacturing and Critical Infrastructure
data.summary: NIST has expanded its collaboration with the nonprofit MITRE Corporation as part of its efforts to ensure U.S. leadership in artificial intelligence.

data.title: NIST Physicists Bring Unruly Molecules to the Quantum Party
data.summary: Molecules can serve as versatile building blocks for quantum technologies, but they are much harder to control than atoms.

data.title: In Quantum Sensing, What Beats Beating Noise? Meeting Noise Halfway.
data.summary: A team including scientists at NIST may have found a new way of dealing with noise at the microscopic scales where quantum physics reigns.

data.title: NIST Ion Clock Sets New Record for Most Accurate Clock in the World
data.summary: This new result contributes to the international effort to define the second with a much greater level of accuracy than before, enabling new scientific and...

data.title: NIST and Partners Use Quantum Mechanics to Make a Factory for Random Numbers
data.summary: Broadcast as a free public service, the beacon can be used anywhere an independent source of random numbers would be useful, such as selecting jury candidates...

data.title: New Atomic Fountain Clock Joins Elite Group That Keeps the World on Time
data.summary: NIST scientists have published results establishing a new atomic clock, NIST-F4, as one of the world’s most accurate timekeepers.

data.title: NIST’s Curved Neutron Beams Could Deliver Benefits Straight to Industry

data.title: NIST Selects HQC as Fifth Algorithm for Post-Quantum Encryption
data.summary: The new algorithm will serve as a backup for the general encryption needed to protect data from quantum computers developed in the future.

data.title: New Atom-Based Thermometer Measures Temperature More Accurately
data.summary: This atomic thermometer provides accurate measurements “out of the box” because it relies on the basic principles of quantum physics.

data.title: Novel ‘Quantum Refrigerator’ Is Great at Erasing Quantum Computer’s Chalkboard
data.summary: Quantum computers need a "clean" workspace, and a team including scientists at NIST has found a way to make one.

data.title: 3 NIST Researchers Receive Presidential Rank Awards
data.summary: All three were awarded the “Distinguished Rank,” the highest category of Presidential Rank Awards.

data.title: NIST Awards $15 Million to ASTM International to Establish Standardization Center of Excellence
data.summary: The new center will support U.S. engagement in international standardization for critical and emerging technologies.

data.title: Major Leap for Nuclear Clock Paves Way for Ultraprecise Timekeeping
data.summary: These clocks could lead to improved timekeeping and navigation, faster internet speeds, and advances in fundamental physics research.

data.title: Tiny New Lasers Fill a Long-Standing Gap in the Rainbow of Visible-Light Colors, Opening New Applications
data.summary: Researchers have created orange, yellow and green lasers tiny enough to fit on a chip.

data.title: NIST Releases First 3 Finalized Post-Quantum Encryption Standards
data.summary: NIST is encouraging computer system administrators to begin transitioning to the new standards as soon as possible.

data.title: NIST Participates in White House Summit on Standards for Critical and Emerging Technology
data.summary: A new Implementation Roadmap provides recommendations and actions for putting the U.S. standards strategy into effect.

data.title: World’s Most Accurate and Precise Atomic Clock Pushes New Frontiers in Physics
data.summary: The clock is the latest demonstration that a much more precise definition of the official second is possible and that new applications of clocks are feasible.

data.title: NIST Awards Over $1.2 Million to Small Businesses to Advance Cybersecurity, Biopharmaceuticals, Semiconductors and More
data.summary: The awards will fund research and development of new products relating to cybersecurity, quantum computing, health care, semiconductor manufacturing and other...

data.title: The Big Quantum Chill: NIST Scientists Modify Common Lab Refrigerator to Cool Faster With Less Energy

data.title: NIST Physicist Sae Woo Nam, Pioneer in Single-Photon Detectors, Passes Away
data.summary: During his 25-year career at NIST, Sae Woo performed groundbreaking work. He died in January at 53.

data.title: Scientists Observe Long-Predicted Superconductor Property Using a Quantum Simulator
data.summary: New insights can help scientists make superconducting materials more robust and useful.

data.title: NIST Team Develops Highest-Resolution Single-Photon Superconducting Camera
data.summary: Researchers at NIST and their colleagues have built a superconducting camera containing 400,000 pixels — 400 times more than any other device of its type.
